title: Dense Algerian Lentil Tagine
description: Savory, glassy-jelly lentil tagine made with sake and a beurre-monte style oil finish, perfect for post-workout nourishment.
instructions: Rinse 200g of lentils under running water until the water runs clear.
Heat 30ml olive oil in a large kazan cauldron over medium heat.
Add the rinsed lentils, 600ml vegetable broth, and 60ml sake to the cauldron, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and cover, simmering for 15 minutes.
Stir in 240ml almond milk, 150g diced tomato, 100g spinach, 1 tsp cumin, and 1 tsp coriander.
Continue simmering uncovered for 10 minutes, until the lentils are tender and the liquid has thickened into a glassy jelly.
Season with 1 tsp sea salt and 1/4 tsp black pepper, stirring until dissolved.
Remove the cauldron from heat and let the tagine rest for 5 minutes to set the glaze.
Serve the dense lentil tagine warm, optionally garnished with a drizzle of olive oil or toasted sesame seeds if desired.
